title: Warbeast Grooming
desc: The royal warbeasts need feeding and grooming. They bite.

steps.text:
The warbeast pens are beneath the oasis, carved into cool stone where the temperature stays constant. The beasts are enormous — scaled, clawed, and deeply uninterested in cooperating with anyone who isn't their assigned handler.

Today's task is grooming. Desert sand works its way under scales, causing irritation that makes the beasts aggressive. An aggressive warbeast is a problem for everyone within biting distance, which is further than you'd think.

The head keeper tosses you a long-handled brush and a bucket of oil. "Start with Skitter. She's the calm one." Skitter is twelve feet long and watching you with the patient intensity of something deciding whether you're food.

"How do I know if she's about to bite?"

"You don't. That's why we use the long brush."

options.label: Start with feeding instead — a full beast is a calm beast

steps.text:
You approach Skitter with the brush extended at full length. She watches you. You watch her. Neither of you blinks.

The first stroke down her flank produces a sound like scraping rust off iron. Sand cascades from between her scales. Skitter's eyes half-close — the universal animal sign for "that feels good, continue or else."

You brush methodically, working oil into the scale gaps. Skitter's tail begins to thump against the stone floor — each thump strong enough to crack tile. The keeper grins. "She likes you. Don't let it go to your head. She liked the last groomer too, right up until she didn't."

By the time you finish, Skitter is practically purring — a deep, subsonic vibration you feel in your chest more than hear. Her scales gleam. She bumps her head against your hand, nearly knocking you off your feet.

"That's affection," the keeper says. "Treasure it. Also, check your fingers. She sometimes takes one as a souvenir."

options.label: Count your fingers and move to the next beast

steps.text:
The feeding stocks are kept in a cold chamber deeper in the catacombs — slabs of salted meat, dried desert lizards, and something labeled "emergency rations" that you decide not to investigate.

You haul a slab of meat to Skitter's pen. She smells it before you round the corner — her head swings toward you, nostrils flaring. You place the meat in the feeding trough. She doesn't move. She stares at you. She stares at the meat. She stares at you again.

"She wants you to leave first," the keeper explains. "They don't eat while being watched. Pride thing."

You step behind a pillar. The sound of feeding is... enthusiastic. Crunching, tearing, a satisfied rumble. When you peek back, the trough is empty and Skitter is grooming herself, scales raised, clearly in a better mood.

The remaining beasts are fed the same way — deliver, retreat, listen to carnage. By the end, you've learned the feeding preferences of six warbeasts and the valuable lesson that "emergency rations" are for the beasts, not the keepers.

The keeper claps you on the back. "Good work. Tomorrow we do claw trimming. Bring armor."

options.label: Make a note to bring armor tomorrow
